title: Aluminum Rally Builds as War Risks Threaten Global Supply Chains
summary: StoneX explains how Middle East disruptions, constrained global production, and China's capacity limits are reshaping the aluminum market, with China approaching its self-imposed 45.5 million ton cap. The analysis suggests China will likely reach this capacity limit within 2026, intensifying global supply concerns.

title: Major US aluminum producer boosts production to full capacity
summary: Century Aluminum has decided to boost production to full capacity amid President Donald Trump's tariffs on aluminum, signaling a strategic response to policy changes. Fox News senior correspondent Steve Harrigan reports this decision as a significant move for US domestic aluminum output.

title: Aluminum prices rise after Iran attacks Gulf smelters
summary: Aluminum prices surged to a four-year high following Iranian attacks on two Gulf smelters, raising fears of a global supply shortage as 9% of global supply originates from the region. Commodities strategist Joyce Li noted that prior to the attacks, capacity was expected to drop by 20%, equating to an 800–900 kiloton production loss in 2026.

title: Latest Steel Prices (June 2026): Hot-Rolled Coil at $1,109/ton
summary: Ryerson reports the latest steel prices for June 2026, with Hot-Rolled Coil at $1,109 per ton, Cold Rolled Steel at $1,293 per ton, and Coated Steel at $1,380 per ton. These figures reflect current market conditions and pricing trends for major steel products.

title: Steel Price Rose to 3067 CNY/T on June 26, 2026
summary: Steel prices increased to 3067 CNY per ton on June 26, 2026, marking a 0.29% rise from the previous day. Although the price has fallen 2.91% over the past month, it remains 4.00% higher than the same period last year.

title: American Steel Just Got More Expensive. Buyers Blame Tariffs.
summary: Cleveland-Cliffs sold steel for an average of $1,015 per ton in the second quarter, up from $980 in the first quarter, as tariffs on steel and aluminum drive price increases. American manufacturers are passing these additional costs onto domestic producers in industries like automotive and military equipment.

title: US Steel Costs Impacted by War, Trade Deals
summary: A video analysis discusses how war and trade deals are impacting US steel costs, with diesel prices rising 50% since the Iran war began. The typical steel pricing upcycle lasts 3–4 months, and the current nine-month march higher suggests prices may continue rising toward $1,500 per ton.

title: Flat-Rolled Steel Prices Update: CR at 1,320–1,360
summary: Steel Market Update provides current flat-rolled steel prices, including Cold Rolled (CR) at 1,320–1,360, Galvanized (GI) at 1,300–1,340, and Aluminum-Zinc (AZ) at 1,300–1,350. These figures reflect recent market fluctuations and pricing trends for flat-rolled steel products.

title: Steel Production Dips in the U.S. | #ScrapMonsterNews
summary: The latest data from AISI shows a 2.4% dip in weekly U.S. raw steel production, with the Southern region still facing challenges. This decline marks a softening in U.S. steel output as reported by ScrapMonsterNews.

title: Steel imports down 30% in 2026 as tariffs bolster US production
summary: Foreign steel entering the United States remains about 30% lower on a year-to-date basis as Section 232 tariffs continue to disrupt trade flows and support domestic steel production. Despite a 6% monthly increase in April, tariffs have significantly reduced overall imports.

title: Global steel industry battles surplus supply, protectionism
summary: Steelmaking capacity is expected to rise for a seventh consecutive year, reaching 2.55 billion metric tons by the end of 2025, according to the OECD steel report. The report pegged 2025 surplus capacity at 680 million mt, with China contending with poor domestic demand and minimal capacity reduction efforts.

title: Current State of the U.S. Steel Market
summary: While demand remains soft in the short term, new production capacity continues to come online driven by anticipated long-term growth. According to AISI, seasonally adjusted output as of April 2025 totaled 24.2 million net tons, down 1.3% from the same period in 2024, with capacity utilization slipping to 74.6%.

title: United States Steel Production
summary: Steel Production in the United States increased to 7500 Thousand Tonnes in May from 7200 Thousand Tonnes in April of 2026. This marks a rebound after production remained unchanged at 7200 Thousand Tonnes in April.

title: Industry Data - American Iron and Steel Institute
summary: In the week ending on June 20, 2026, domestic raw steel production was 1,851,000 net tons while the capability utilization rate was 80.2 percent. This indicates a strong utilization rate approaching the administration's stated goal of 80.0%.

title: Nickel Prices Ready to Rise as Supply Tightens in Fourth Quarter
summary: The global nickel market is experiencing a critical inflection point as supply and demand dynamics converge, potentially ending the prolonged range-bound pricing between $15,000-$15,800 per ton that persisted since April 2025. Indonesia's strategic shift represents the most significant development in nickel markets, signaling a new reality for the industry.

title: Nickel Futures Price Today - Investing.com
summary: The current price of Nickel futures is 16,860.88, with a previous close of 16,860.88, while the spot price today is 16,852.88. Market data shows slight fluctuations with the current futures price at 16,835.63 compared to a previous close of 16,801.13.

title: Nickel Prices 2026 - Chart, Trends Analysis & Forecast - IMARC Group
summary: Nickel prices in Northeast Asia reached USD 21.45/kg in May 2026, up 13.3%, while Europe saw prices at USD 19.58/kg, up 19.5%. This marks a significant increase from April 2026, where prices were 18.93 USD/Kg in Northeast Asia and 16.39 USD/Kg in Europe.

title: Nickel Below $17K: Macro Pressure, Not Fundamental Weakness
summary: Nickel prices have slipped below $17,000 per tonne in June 2026, down from the $18,500–$20,000 range seen earlier. The decline is attributed to macroeconomic pressure rather than fundamental weakness in the nickel market.

title: Nickel fell to $16,700 per tonne, nearing its lowest level since late December
summary: Nickel fell to $16,700 per tonne, nearing its lowest level since late December, as Indonesia prepares to permit a substantial increase in nickel mine output. This potential supply surge is contributing to the downward pressure on prices.

title: World's Biggest Nickel Mine in Indonesia Told to Slash Output
summary: The world's largest nickel mine in Indonesia, PT Weda Bay Nickel, has been notified to drastically cut its ore production quota from 42 million tons in 2025 to 12 million tons this year to boost global prices. The company intends to apply for a revision of this decision, which has already caused nickel prices to climb as the metal is critical for stainless steel and electric-vehicle batteries.

title: Nickel price spikes as risks to supply grow in top producer Indonesia
summary: Nickel prices rose 1.3% to $18,806 a ton on the LME following reports of further output cuts in Indonesia, where rotational maintenance will affect 10% to 15% of high-grade nickel pig iron capacity at the Weda Bay Industrial Park. Some Indonesian production of nickel pig iron has already been curtailed since March due to dropping ore supplies and high costs.

title: Indonesia navigates nickel market with output cuts, policy shifts
summary: Indonesia confirmed plans to cut nickel production in 2026 to support prices and government revenue while cracking down on environmentally harmful operations. The country is reversing its mining quota policy to regulate supply volatility, with actual production currently lagging behind potential capacity.

title: Aosta, Cogne Acciai Speciali: bilancio in rosso e futuro incerto
summary: Cogne Acciai Speciali ha registrato una perdita di 60,9 milioni di euro nel 2025, in netto peggioramento rispetto all'utile di 8 milioni dell'anno precedente. L'azienda affronta una situazione complessa dovuta a dazi statunitensi, concorrenza asiatica e rincari dell'energia, con preoccupazioni da parte di CGil e Fiom per le prospettive future.
query: Cogne Acciai Speciali news aluminum steel italy

title: Torna la cassa integrazione in Cogne Acciai Speciali
summary: La cassa integrazione torna in Cogne Acciai Speciali, coinvolgendo 750 dipendenti per 13 settimane dal 9 giugno al 7 settembre. Questa misura riflette le difficoltà operative dell'azienda in un contesto di crescente pressione economica e concorrenza internazionale.
query: Cogne Acciai Speciali news aluminum steel italy

title: Italy's Prysmian sees stronger U.S. pricing, margins after new copper tariff scheme
summary: Prysmian, an Italian cable manufacturer, is expected to benefit from the new copper tariff framework in the United States that imposes duties on imported cables. The company's CEO stated that the new levies on foreign-made cables will strengthen U.S. pricing and margins for the group.

title: Prysmian plans New York listing and more acquisitions in US
summary: The Italian cable company Prysmian is planning a New York stock listing and further acquisitions in the United States to insulate itself from potential trade tensions. The company aims to establish a manufacturing base in America to reduce exposure to tariffs and regulatory risks.

title: Prysmian receives the notice to proceed for the Italy-Tunisia electrical interconnector
summary: Prysmian has been officially notified to proceed with the construction of the Italy-Tunisia electrical interconnector, a major energy infrastructure project. This development underscores Prysmian's role in advancing cross-border energy connectivity between Europe and Africa.
